steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that Venus has whistler waves with energy signatures like Earth's (which come from lightning), yet observations of other lightning-consistent phenomena — like light flashes — are rare. The question asks which choice best explains this mismatch.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — there are geophysical characteristics of Venus not shared with Earth that promote the generation of whistler waves.: explains why whistler waves exist without visible lightning phenomena. ✓
  • Choice B — the purported Venusian whistler waves must actually be some other type of atmospheric activity than whistler waves.: contradicts the passage, which states they are similar. ✗
  • Choice C — Venusian lightning has properties that make it unlikely to generate whistler waves.: contradicts established science the passage relies on. ✗
  • Choice D — similarities in the energy signatures of Venusian and Earth whistler waves may reflect imprecisions in the PVO and VEX data.: invokes error rather than explaining the underlying phenomenon. ✗
That matches Choice A — there are geophysical characteristics of Venus not shared with Earth that promote the generation of whistler waves..
